summary: GitHub Actions' job-log endpoints (both the REST API and the public HTML commit-checks log-viewer path) were tested for genuinely credential-free, anonymous downloadability and confirmed NOT reproducibly acquirable for the 3 native-upstream-ci-log candidates originally selected on GitHub Actions. The 3 candidates were replaced with real, verified AppVeyor-hosted equivalents whose log endpoint IS confirmed anonymously downloadable. The native-upstream-ci-log = 3 quota and the credential-free acquisition rule were both preserved unchanged.
background: The original acquisition design attached this workflow's own GITHUB_TOKEN to api.github.com requests for the job-logs endpoint, reasoning that GitHub's job-logs download endpoint returns 403 for a fully unauthenticated request even against a public repository (real CI run 29393029603).

This was challenged: the token used was scoped to a different repository (PhysShell/007), so a 401/403 response does not by itself prove genuine anonymous access is impossible — an authenticated-but-wrong-scope request behaves differently from a truly anonymous one, and the original test also omitted the X-GitHub-Api-Version header.

An independently-verified working reference was supplied: jqlang/jq, run_id 28568334149, job_id 84700257505, job name 'linux (amd64)' — real log bytes had previously been retrieved for this exact job. It was later established that this retrieval went through an installed GitHub App (i.e. an authenticated path), which proves the job's log bytes exist and are retrievable via SOME credentialed path, but does not by itself establish anonymous availability.

probes.method: Plain curl GET against https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/jobs/{job_id}/logs with Accept: application/vnd.github+json and X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28 only. GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_AUTH_TOKEN, GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_TOKEN explicitly unset. No Authorization header constructed or sent.
probes.anonymity_corroboration: x-ratelimit-limit: 60 on every response (GitHub's unauthenticated per-IP tier; an authenticated request would show 5000+).
probes.conclusion: All 3 jobs, including the independently-verified reference, returned HTTP 403 under a genuinely anonymous, correctly-headed request. No redirects occurred (redirect_hop_count: 0 for all three).

probes.method: Plain curl GET against github.com/{owner}/{repo}/commit/{head_sha}/checks/{job_id}/logs (the URL GitHub's own Actions web UI uses for public-repo 'raw logs'/'download log archive' links), with no cookie jar, no browser profile, no session of any kind, and no Authorization header. head_sha resolved via the already-anonymous run-metadata REST endpoint.

results.label: jq-html-viewer-84700257505
results.note: GitHub's standard 404 HTML page, not real log content

results.label: curl-html-viewer-87202088495
results.note: GitHub's standard 404 HTML page, not real log content

probes.conclusion: Both tested jobs, including the independently-verified reference, returned HTTP 404 (GitHub's standard not-found page) under a genuinely anonymous request. No redirects occurred.

decision: The plain anonymous GitHub REST job-logs endpoint and the public HTML commit-checks log-viewer path are both falsified as anonymous acquisition paths for these 3 specific candidates. This does not amend the native-upstream-ci-log origin-kind requirement itself, does not change the quota (still 3), and does not weaken the credential-free acquisition rule. ci-log-curl-linux, ci-log-jq-ci, and ci-log-rust-lang-rust-ci are marked ineligible (requires_interactive_access_grant: true) with this evidence attached as acquisition_barrier_evidence.

replacement_candidates.endpoint_verified_anonymous: https://ci.appveyor.com/api/buildjobs/{job_id}/log — confirmed during N2-C closure research to return real runner output (git clone, cmake/msbuild build steps) via a plain, zero-credential HTTP GET for a public project, HTTP 200, content-type application/octet-stream.
replacement_candidates.note: Local verification during discovery research confirms the anonymous path works and the deterministic job-selection rule is well-defined; the authoritative source_content_sha256/normalized_source_sha256 values locked into the registry come from the real trusted-source-acquisition CI job (identity-lock commit), not from this local research fetch, per the two-phase identity-lock process (section 5).

job_selection_rule_appveyor: max(job.finished - job.started), tie-break lexicographically lowest job id (AppVeyor job ids are opaque alphanumeric strings, not numeric — same structure-only rule as the GitHub Actions rule, adapted for the id format).

ecosystem_minimum_followup.problem: Marking ci-log-rust-lang-rust-ci ineligible removed its incidental ecosystem="rust" tag, which had been the only slack quota-contract.json's ecosystem-minimum-reconciliation swap (selection.py) relied on to satisfy the dotnet:2 minimum (swapping repo-moq in for repo-rustlings in the repository-miner::test bucket). With that tag gone, rust/jvm/python were each exactly at their minimum (3, zero slack), so only 1 of 2 needed dotnet swaps could succeed and selection.py reported status=QUOTA_CHECK_FAILED (dotnet actual 1, target 2).
ecosystem_minimum_followup.search: Extensively searched AppVeyor (~30 real projects) for an actively-maintained Rust project to honestly restore the rust-ecosystem slack; none exists — every Rust crate that ever used AppVeyor abandoned it years before this investigation (all either 404 or stale since 2019-2021). Fabricating a rust tag on a C/C++ project, or lowering quota-contract.json's dotnet minimum, were both explicitly ruled out as unacceptable fixes.
ecosystem_minimum_followup.resolution: The correct fix was a genuine ecosystem=dotnet candidate in the native-upstream-ci-log::ci-build bucket itself, not a rust-ecosystem substitute. Found and verified NLog/NLog (https://github.com/NLog/NLog, BSD-3-Clause, AppVeyor build 54377650, commit 736854b49cad342a98ce0e56f59c94914e0b555c) — a real, actively-maintained .NET logging library with a build from 2026-07-14, anonymous log fetch confirmed (HTTP 200, real dotnet/MSBuild build output, 159003 bytes, sha256 89f3dcc56160c7ec748df00103f079504a3f4b1375da1928c389314dbdedd87a for the deterministically-selected job nh2g5c8x06txldor). Added as a 4th eligible candidate (ci-log-nlog) alongside the 3 existing AppVeyor C/C++ candidates, without forcing it into the primary set.
ecosystem_minimum_followup.outcome: Rerunning the unmodified deterministic selection + ecosystem-reconciliation algorithm against all 4 eligible candidates naturally selected ci-log-nlog as primary and moved ci-log-nlohmann-json to alternate (fallback_priority 1) — a genuine swap, not a manual substitution. Final state: status=FINAL, dotnet=2, rust=3, all origin-kind/primary-family targets exactly met, unmet_ecosystem_minimums={}. ci-log-nlohmann-json remains eligible and is preserved as the top alternate.

workflow_infrastructure_note: probe-ci-log-anonymous-access and probe-ci-log-html-viewer-access were temporary investigation-only workflow jobs, not part of the frozen final design. Both are removed from qodec-n2-source-freeze.yml now that this decision record captures their full evidence; the real, permanent AppVeyor acquisition path lives in run_acquisition.py's _acquire_ci_run_appveyor.
